LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY IDENTIFIED THE ACCOUNT NUMBER AT PAGE 4 OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND STATES THAT THE ACCOUNT IS HELD BY DEFENDANT AT PAGE 5. WHETHER THE ACCOUNT IS IN FACT HELD BY DEFENDANT IS A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT CANNOT BE DETERMINED ON DEMURRER AND WHICH DEFENDANT IS FREE TO DENY IN ITS ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Continued To Jun-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jason Julian, Esq. of Severson & Werson, Tel. 415.677.5552, for defendant and moving party;
John Rafael Aponte, plaintiff appearing on his own behalf by telephone.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was not contested. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently identified the account number at page 4 of the First Amended Complaint and states that the account is held by Defendant at page 5. Whether the account is in fact held by Defendant is a factual issue that cannot be determined on demurrer and which Defendant is free to deny in its answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
